item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually no, I haven't read 312 until you mentioned it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get that you want to hit on a fear, especially one that may have hit close to home but there's little here to personalize that fear. Its random, indiscriminate and rare. This would be more sinister, creepy and worrisome if there was a hint to a source or intelligence doing this.

Odds of this thing throwing you to the sky is 1 in ~3 billion (or total number of people outside, and on the planet). Odds of getting hit by a car and killed, 1 in ~42,000. So for an odds standpoint i'm more terrified of coconuts falling on me.

Hypoxia will start hitting around 5000m, take less than two minutes to fall up to that point. Another minute and there won't be enough oxygen to think clearly at all. It would be nice if your video showed this. A victim will lose their mind, probably think everything is ok, fine, and just want to sleep. But reality is their brain is dying and can't put together they are being strangled and frozen to death. They won't be conscious past the 12 km point. (Edge of atmos is 100 km. Being anti-gravity'd to death is a cruel and fairly quick death.

Just some thoughts! Good on you for taking your fear and trying to make it into something!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also a first thought was “This seems really similar to 312” though that’s probably your intention since the last 3 digits match? If so, forgive me for being dumb lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally I feel as though there should be more to it’s cause and/or existence than it just happening randomly to someone anywhere

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Going up? Do you have a choice?

item.title: Minor changes
item.content:encoded:
I made some edits to the conprocs, as the old ones were lackluster and unfit for the tone of the piece (in case y’all missed it, I had some stupid afterthought about faking a human balloon hobbyist movement.)

Also fixed some clunky description wording.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A fantastic example of great flash fiction.

item.title: Re: Deep Authorial Post
item.content:encoded: i read the collapsible "indubitably" and thought of the baby is you and then i see the fucking sbahj reference and im losing it, skip was good too lmao

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article came in, punched me in the gut, then ran away.
Short, snappy, and extremely unnerving.

item.title: Deep Authorial Post
item.content:encoded:
+ Deep authory stuff
- hmmmm yes I am an author yes indubitably 

This originated from an unreasonable fear that I had as a little kid, where I was terrified that gravity would switch for no reason. I ended up combining this with the feeling that people can just die by random chance in the SCPverse and ended up with this. Small-scale, personal, and it terrifies the shit out of me that this could happen to someone without warning.

I keep chuckling to myself at the thought of replacing that final image with "are you next…? ARE YOU NEXT???" in scarlet letters and a blooddrip font, but that would kinda ruin everything.

item.title: Credits
item.content:encoded:
Shoutout to Slifar, Humanmale, and The Great Hippo for critique. Additional shoutout to Hippo for doing the image for the final frame for me. The original for that can be found here and is in public domain.

The other image is under a Creative Commons 4.0 License, and the bottom bit was cropped.

EDIT: I decided to remove the second image mentioned, as it has a completely different look from the second one. Still leaving the image credit though, as it is a nice picture which helped me with the creative process.
